title: User mode networking fails after a while
body:
### Issue Description

After having restarted my machine, I had Podman Desktop waiting forever for my Podman machine being started.
Using the process manager, I noticed that there were 2 podman processes out there: the one to start the podman machine and another to list the podman machine. So it seems to me there was kind of a deadlock between them.

So I decided to test a similar scenario but out of Podman Desktop.
So I setup 2 shells that were:

```
while true
do
  podman machine ls --format json
done

```
and

```
while true
do
  podman machine start
  podman machine stop
done
```

After a while, I have this error message:

```
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Starting user-mode networking...
Error: error setting up user-mode networking: route state is missing a default route: shutdown all machines and run wsl --shutdown to recover
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Launch first script
2. Launch second script
3. Wait a few minutes


### Describe the results you received

```
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Starting user-mode networking...
Error: error setting up user-mode networking: route state is missing a default route: shutdown all machines and run wsl --shutdown to recover
```

### Describe the results you expected

Machine should start forever

### podman info output

```yaml
podman.exe version 4.6.1
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: Thanks for the report @jeffmaury. I have a theory on where there may be a race, will take a look
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I'm facing the same error, whenver I start my podman machine
